column1: # Dependency graph for Layer 6 (LowExpr-consuming emit) substrate chunks.
# Each row declares one chunk + its tier + its Hβ-emit-substrate.md §
# citation + its imports (must be defined in priors). build.sh assembles
# per this order; future drift-audit verifies imports resolve before
# chunk lands. Mirror of bootstrap/src/lower/INDEX.tsv discipline.
#   §3.5 — EmitMemory swap surface (state.wat is substrate-level reference;
# Per the eight interrogations on Hβ.emit structure (mirroring the
# lower/INDEX.tsv discipline at the emit layer):
#   Graph?       — this file IS the dep graph for Layer 6 (LowExpr-consuming) chunks
#   Handler?     — build.sh is the assembler-handler reading this
#   Verb?        — chunk concatenation is `|>` per row order
#   Row?         — each chunk's "uses" column declares its symbol-row
#   Ownership?   — each chunk OWNS its exports; consumers `ref` them
#   Refinement?  — Hβ-emit-substrate.md § cite IS the per-chunk
#   Gradient?    — each row added unlocks one emission capability
#   Reason?      — hβ_section + commit message records why each landed
# Tiers (dependency depth — Layer 6 sits atop Layer 1 runtime + Layer 4
# infer + Layer 5 lower; see runtime/INDEX.tsv + infer/INDEX.tsv +
#   4 — depends only on Layer 1 runtime (no other emit-layer deps)
#   5 — depends on Tier 4 + Layer 1 runtime + Layer 4 infer (ty.wat) +
#   6 — depends on Tier 5 + Tier 4 + lower-layer + infer
#   7 — depends on Tier 6 + ... per §11.3 dep order
# / main.wat) get appended as their chunks land per
# Hβ-emit-substrate.md §11.3 dep order. NO placeholder rows for
# unlanded chunks per the residue-tracker convention.
